corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: The key insight behind our new architecture, as illustrated in Figure , is that for many states, it is unnecessary to estimate the value of each action choice.
To bring this insight to fruition, we design a single Qnetwork architecture, as illustrated in Figure , which we refer to as the dueling network.
To bring this insight to fruition, we design a single Qnetwork architecture, as illustrated in Figure , which we refer to as the dueling network.
The module that combines the two streams of fullyconnected layers to output a Q estimate requires very thoughtful design.

As the dueling architecture shares the same input-output interface with standard Q networks, we can recycle all learning algorithms with Q networks (e.g., DDQN and SARSA) to train the dueling architecture.
In this paper, we present a new neural network architecture for model-free reinforcement learning.
In this paper, we present a new neural network architecture for model-free reinforcement learning.
The main benefit of this factoring is to generalize learning across actions without imposing any change to the underlying reinforcement learning algorithm.
Our results show that this architecture leads to better policy evaluation in the presence of many similar-valued actions.
Moreover, the dueling architecture enables our RL agent to outperform the state-of-the-art on the Atari 2600 domain.
Moreover, the dueling architecture enables our RL agent to outperform the state-of-the-art on the Atari 2600 domain.
Moreover, the dueling architecture enables our RL agent to outperform the state-of-the-art on the Atari 2600 domain.
Here, we take an alternative but complementary approach of focusing primarily on innovating a neural network architecture that is better suited for model-free RL.
The proposed network architecture, which we name the dueling architecture, explicitly separates the representation of state values and (state-dependent) action advantages.
The dueling architecture consists of two streams that represent the value and advantage functions, while sharing a common Figure .
Intuitively, the dueling architecture can learn which states are (or are not) valuable, without having to learn the effect of each action for each state.

In the experiments, we demonstrate that the dueling architecture can more quickly identify the correct action during policy evaluation as redundant or similar actions are added to the learning problem.
We also evaluate the gains brought in by the dueling architecture on the challenging Atari 2600 testbed.
The combination of prioritized replay with the proposed dueling network results in the new state-of-the-art for this popular domain.
See, attend and drive: Value and advantage saliency maps (red-tinted overlay) on the Atari game Enduro, for a trained dueling architecture.

The advantage stream learns to pay attention only when there are cars immediately in front, so as to avoid collisions. advantage updating algorithm, the shared Bellman residual update equation is decomposed into two updates: one for a state value function, and one for its associated advantage function.
Its successor, the advantage learning algorithm, represents only a single advantage function .
The dueling architecture represents both the value V (s) and advantage A(s, a) functions with a single deep model whose output combines the two to produce a state-action value Q(s, a).
Unlike in advantage updating, the representation and algorithm are decoupled by construction.
Consequently, the dueling architecture can be used in combination with a myriad of model free RL algorithms.
